title: profile-controller (metacontroller CompositeController) broken: 'namespaces. not found' -> no per-namespace reconciliation
description: metacontroller-0 fails EVERY sync of the kubeflow-pipelines-profile-controller CompositeController with: 'Reconciler error ... CustomResourceDefinition.apiextensions.k8s.io "namespaces." not found'. The CC parentResource is core {apiVersion: v1, resource: namespaces}; this metacontroller build (controller-runtime v0.19.3) appears unable to resolve the core namespaces resource and treats it as a missing CRD. IMPACT: per-profile-namespace KFP resources (ml-pipeline-ui-artifact, ml-pipeline-visualizationserver, secrets, configmaps, services, destinationrules, authorizationpolicies) are NEVER reconciled — they were stuck at an old image (frontend:2.3) until manually replaced, and any new profile namespace would get no/stale managed resources. This is foundational to multi-user. Webhook itself works (POST /sync returns correct 2.16.1 manifests); the break is metacontroller resolving the parent resource. NEXT: check metacontroller version vs the CompositeController parentResource spec (may need apiVersion/resource form change, an updated metacontroller, or RBAC/discovery fix); compare against a known-good KFP+metacontroller pairing.
acceptance_criteria: metacontroller reconciles the profile-controller CompositeController without error; deleting a managed per-namespace child (e.g. ml-pipeline-ui-artifact) results in automatic recreation at the configured image.
notes: FIX A IMPLEMENTED (chart, uncommitted): replaced templates/composite_controller.yaml with templates/decorator_controller.yaml (kind DecoratorController; resources: namespaces with labelSelector pipelines.kubeflow.org/enabled=true; attachments: secrets/configmaps/deployments/services/destinationrules/authorizationpolicies; same /sync webhook). Surgically migrated the sync.py in config_maps.yaml to the DecoratorController interface: def sync(self, parent, attachments); do_POST reads observed['object']/observed['attachments']; returns {status, attachments}; status block uses attachments[...]. All local customizations preserved (x-forwarded-email etc.). Bumped pipelines chart 1.1.5->1.1.6 + tf pin. DecoratorController CRD already present on cluster. helm template renders clean (DecoratorController:1, CompositeController:0). NOT yet deployed. On deploy: Helm creates the DecoratorController, prunes the old CompositeController, updates the sync configmap; restart the profile-controller webhook to load new sync.py. metacontroller v4.11.22 supports DecoratorController with built-in (namespaces) resources, so reconciliation should resume. VERIFY post-deploy: metacontroller-0 logs show no 'namespaces. not found'; deleting a per-namespace child auto-recreates it.
close_reason: FIXED & VALIDATED LIVE. Migrated profile-controller CompositeController->DecoratorController (+ sync.py object/attachments interface, + checksum annotation, pipelines chart 1.1.6). User deployed via terraform. Post-deploy: DecoratorController present, CompositeController pruned, webhook restarted with new sync.py. metacontroller: 0 reconciler errors (was 100% 'namespaces. not found'). Reconciliation confirmed: deleting ml-pipeline-ui-artifact auto-recreates it in ~4s at ghcr.io/kubeflow/kfp-frontend:2.16.1 (ready), now controller-owned. Both per-namespace pods (artifact+viz) managed at 2.16.1. Root cause was the v2.0.5->v4.11.22 metacontroller bump (during pipelines upgrade) without the matching upstream Composite->Decorator migration.

title: SeaweedFS admin-user name collides with kubeflow-admin profile namespace
description: platform-wiz defect found in live validation on archegos-dev-eks. SeaweedFS admin user is hardcoded 'kubeflow-admin' in the seaweedfs Deployment postStart (s3.configure -user kubeflow-admin -actions Admin). The admin profile namespace is also 'kubeflow-admin'. The profile-controller mints per-tenant S3 creds keyed by namespace name (iam.create_access_key/put_user_policy UserName=<namespace>), so reconciling the kubeflow-admin namespace operates on the shared admin identity: the round-1 prune-then-create deletes the admin access key and put_user_policy downgrades Admin->scoped, invalidating the central artifact credential (api-server/UI). Recurs on every reconcile (hourly resync + any ns change). FIX (chart 1.3.0): rename admin user kubeflow-admin -> seaweedfs-admin in postStart (deployments.yaml); auth is by access key so the name is internal-only. Needs one-time live remediation after deploy: reset seaweedfs identity store (recreate PVC or s3.configure -delete -user kubeflow-admin), delete stale kubeflow-admin ns secret+kfp-launcher so they recreate with scoped key + private-artifacts root, scale profile-controller back to 1. Acceptance: two identities (seaweedfs-admin=Admin, kubeflow-admin=scoped); kubeflow-admin ns secret accesskey != central admin key; reconcile no longer touches the admin identity; sample v2 pipeline SUCCEEDS with artifacts under private-artifacts/kubeflow-admin/.
close_reason: Fixed in chart 1.3.0: renamed SeaweedFS admin user kubeflow-admin -> seaweedfs-admin in the postStart so the admin identity no longer collides with the kubeflow-admin profile namespace (auth is by access key, name is internal-only). Deployed to archegos-dev-eks + one-time remediation: restored seaweedfs-admin (Admin, central key), cleared stale kubeflow-admin per-ns secret/kfp-launcher/artifact-repositories, let the controller re-provision. Validated end-to-end: two distinct identities (seaweedfs-admin=Admin matching central secret; kubeflow-admin=scoped with its own key 2RKR...); reconciles idempotent (no admin clobber/key churn); a real KFP v2 pipeline ran SUCCEEDED with artifacts written under private-artifacts/kubeflow-admin/ (scoped key + prefixed LIST confirmed, bucket top-level only private-artifacts = isolation). Validation pipeline kept at examples/pipelines/seaweedfs_artifact_check.py.

title: Regression: KFP per-profile artifact preview (ml-pipeline-ui-artifact) 500s on freshly-created pods
description: Artifact preview in the Pipelines UI for runs in kubeflow-admin returns 500 'Failed to initialize Minio Client for Minio Provider: Encountered error when trying to fetch provider secret mlpipeline-minio-artifact'. The pipeline RUN itself succeeds; only the UI artifact preview fails. Served by the per-profile ml-pipeline-ui-artifact deployment (created by the pipelines sync.py composite controller) in kubeflow-admin. ORIGIN: surfaced while investigating an 'occasional' preview error; the original 3-day-old pod worked sequentially (failed only under concurrent burst), but a pod restart during investigation triggered a CONSISTENT failure on every freshly-created pod.
design: RULED OUT with evidence: (1) secret/keys/RBAC/connectivity fine — manual SA-token GET of the secret from inside the pod returns 200, minio reachable (200); (2) CPU limit (sync.py sets 100m) — bumping live to 500m did NOT fix it; (3) sidecar vs ambient — a freshly-created pod WITH a sidecar also 500s; (4) waypoint — removing istio.io/use-waypoint from the ns did NOT fix it. PATTERN: only the original ~3d-old pod worked; EVERY pod created during/after this session fails. The failure is internal to the webpack-bundled KFP artifact node app's secret/minio-client init (couldn't replicate via @kubernetes/client-node — MODULE_NOT_FOUND on the bundled server). Something environmental that changed between when the old pod was created (~3d ago) and now makes fresh pods' app-level fetch fail, while raw k8s/minio calls from the same pod succeed. NEXT: get the actual wrapped exception (e.g. run KFP frontend with verbose logging or read frontend source for what 'fetch provider secret' wraps); compare old-pod vs new-pod env/spec; consider this likely affects ml-pipeline-visualizationserver too. Likely tied to the multi-user-ambient profile-namespace dataplane story (platform-lyl, kubeflow-dataplane-split).
acceptance_criteria: A freshly-created ml-pipeline-ui-artifact pod in kubeflow-admin serves /artifacts/get with HTTP 200; artifact previews render in the UI for a profile-namespace run.
notes: ROOT CAUSE FOUND & FIX VALIDATED. Captured the swallowed error by running a 2nd patched frontend instance on port 3001: 'secrets mlpipeline-minio-artifact is forbidden: User system:serviceaccount:kubeflow-admin:default-editor cannot get secrets in namespace KUBEFLOW' (HTTP 403) at getK8sSecret (k8s-helper.js:296) <- parseS3ProviderInfo <- createMinioClient. ROOT CAUSE: the per-profile artifact proxy (ml-pipeline-ui-artifact in kubeflow-admin) reads the mlpipeline-minio-artifact secret from the WRONG namespace 'kubeflow' instead of its own 'kubeflow-admin'. Why: the main ml-pipeline-ui proxy does NOT forward the namespace query param, so the artifact server falls back to options.server.serverNamespace = FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE env, which is UNSET on our artifact deployment -> frontend defaults to 'kubeflow'. default-editor (kubeflow-admin) can't read secrets cross-ns in kubeflow -> 403 -> swallowed by minio-helper.ts catch -> generic 'fetch provider secret' 500. NOT ambient/version/CPU/waypoint related (all earlier theories were red herrings; my isolated getK8sSecret tests passed only because I explicitly passed namespace=kubeflow-admin, while the server used the kubeflow default). FIX (upstream-aligned, KFP sync.py sets this at line 401): added env FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE: namespace to the artifact deployment in our sync.py (config_maps.yaml); pipelines chart 1.1.6->1.1.7 + tf pin. VALIDATED LIVE: ran a 2nd frontend with FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE=kubeflow-admin -> the exact failing request returns HTTP 200 (was 500).
close_reason: FIXED & VALIDATED on deployed cluster. Root cause: per-profile artifact proxy read mlpipeline-minio-artifact from namespace 'kubeflow' (default) instead of its own 'kubeflow-admin' because FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE was unset -> default-editor cross-ns 403 -> swallowed -> generic 'fetch provider secret' 500. Captured via patched 2nd frontend instance. Fix: added FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE: namespace to the artifact deployment in sync.py (matches upstream KFP), pipelines 1.1.7. After deploy + a DecoratorController re-sync, the artifact pod has FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE=kubeflow-admin and /artifacts/get returns HTTP 200 (seq + concurrent) with content. NOT ambient/version/CPU/waypoint (all red herrings). NOTE: existing profile namespaces pick up sync.py changes on the next DecoratorController resync (hourly) or a namespace event; I forced it via a namespace annotation.

title: KFP multi-user: align userid header to X-Forwarded-Email (oauth2-proxy reverse-proxy)
description: Browsing the Pipelines page (namespace=kubeflow-admin) returned api-server error code 16 'User identity is empty in the request header'. Root cause: header-name mismatch. oauth2-proxy runs in REVERSE-PROXY mode; set_xauthrequest only sets X-Auth-Request-* as auth_request RESPONSE headers, while pass_user_headers sends X-Forwarded-Email upstream. KFP (api-server, ml-pipeline-ui, persistenceagent, scheduledworkflow), the profile-controller, and the waypoint policy all read x-auth-request-email -> never present -> 401. Surfaced only now because MULTIUSER mode REQUIRES the identity header. Verified: ml-pipeline-ui + api-server authorize correctly WHEN the header they read is present (direct port-forward test); oauth2-proxy authenticates the user (logs admin@aedenjameson.com) but doesn't deliver x-auth-request-email upstream. Considered Option B (oauth2-proxy alpha injectRequestHeaders to emit X-Auth-Request-Email) but it's a risky, headless-unverifiable full-config rewrite that breaks all login if wrong. Chose Option A (user-confirmed): align KFP to X-Forwarded-Email, the header oauth2-proxy reliably emits. Precedent: awslabs/kubeflow-manifests uses kubeflow-userid + an ingressgateway EnvoyFilter/Lua to inject it (ALB+Cognito, no oauth2-proxy) — validates header-injection pattern but doesn't drop into our reverse-proxy+ambient setup.
acceptance_criteria: After deploy: browsing the Pipelines page as the namespace owner lists pipelines/runs in kubeflow-admin (no code-16 'user identity is empty'); a no-SA pipeline run still SUCCEEDS; non-owner denied.
notes: PR #181 Copilot feedback addressed (1a+2a): (1a) ns-owner-access-waypoint.yaml now lowercases the Istio key via {{ .Values.userid_header | lower }} — Envoy stores header names lowercase and Istio matches case-sensitively, so the prior request.headers[X-Forwarded-Email] would never match and would break waypoint owner-isolation; renders as request.headers[x-forwarded-email] now. Bumped kubeflow-profiles 1.0.4->1.0.5 (no tf pin; provider reads Chart.yaml). (2a) Removed dead userid_header from oauth2-proxy/values.yaml + the terraform oauth2-proxy release block (no oauth2-proxy template consumed it; it emits headers, doesn't read one). Remaining terraform userid_header refs (profiles, dashboard releases) are legitimate consumers. All charts render.
close_reason: Deployed + verified: Pipelines page loads in browser; api-server authorizes namespace-scoped queries with x-forwarded-email (no code-16); no-SA pipeline run tutorial-data-passing-cfz4g Succeeded in kubeflow-admin (no regression). Live waypoint policy key is lowercase request.headers[x-forwarded-email] + status Accepted. RESIDUAL (not blocking): non-owner-denied functional test needs a second authenticated user/browser; structural enforcement confirmed (waypoint Accepted + active email when-clause).

title: Multi-user mesh authz: pipeline backends deny profile-namespace default-editor callers
description: platform-02x multi-user E2E (2026-06-24): a no-SA run now correctly lands in kubeflow-admin and step pods run as default-editor (routing + SA + RBAC all verified working after platform-38t ClusterRoles + kfp-launcher stopgap). But the run FAILS: the KFP driver pod (kubeflow-admin/sa/default-editor) calling ml-pipeline gRPC (:8887, MLMD/cache) gets 'connection reset by peer' — an Istio AuthorizationPolicy denial. The ml-pipeline AuthorizationPolicy (pipelines chart authorization_policies.yaml) only allows 6 kubeflow-ns SAs (ml-pipeline, ml-pipeline-ui, persistenceagent, scheduledworkflow, viewer-crd, kubeflow-pipelines-cache); its 2nd rule (when x-auth-request-email) is L7 and silently dropped by ztunnel (kubeflow ns is ambient). The profile-namespace pipeline runner (cluster.local/ns/kubeflow-admin/sa/default-editor) is not allowed. Same gap likely affects metadata-grpc-service, minio-service, mysql, ml-pipeline-visualizationserver, service-cache-server when called from profile-namespace step pods. ROOT THEME: these mesh authz policies were written for single-user (all callers in kubeflow ns); multi-user step pods run in profile namespaces. FIX: allow the profile-namespace pipeline runner principal on the relevant policies — e.g. add cluster.local/ns/*/sa/default-editor (wildcard ns) to ml-pipeline + metadata-grpc + minio + mysql + viz + cache, or enumerate profile namespaces. Overlaps platform-f2i (minio/metadata scoping) and platform-a0v. BLOCKS platform-02x.
acceptance_criteria: A no-SA pipeline run in a profile namespace (kubeflow-admin) completes Succeeded; driver/launcher/step pods reach ml-pipeline, metadata-grpc, and minio without Istio RBAC resets.
notes: ROOT CAUSE (not a missing principal) 2026-06-24: KFP driver/step pods set sidecar.istio.io/inject=false. In MULTI-user they run in profile namespaces (kubeflow-admin) which are SIDECAR mode (istio-injection=enabled, NO dataplane-mode=ambient). inject=false + non-ambient ns => pod gets NEITHER a sidecar NOR ambient capture => NO mesh identity at all. ztunnel sees the connection to ml-pipeline (ambient) with no authenticated principal and rejects ('allow policies exist, but none allowed'). PROVEN: tried middle-wildcard, suffix-wildcard, AND exact cluster.local/ns/kubeflow-admin/sa/default-editor principals on the ml-pipeline policy — ALL rejected, because the source has no identity to match (also confirmed ztunnel only honors exact principals, no string wildcards). CONTRAST: single-user works because step pods run in kubeflow (ambient) where istio-cni captures them despite inject=false, giving a ztunnel identity. FIX (architectural): profile namespaces must be AMBIENT so istio-cni captures inject=false step pods and assigns identity; THEN add the step-pod principal (cluster.local/ns/<profile-ns>/sa/default-editor, exact) to ml-pipeline + metadata-grpc + minio + mysql policies. Making profile ns ambient drops their L7 ns-owner-access-istio enforcement -> REQUIRES waypoints (platform-xd4). Reverted all speculative ml-pipeline patches to deployed state. This issue now depends on the profile-ns dataplane decision + platform-xd4.
close_reason: VERIFIED: profile-ns ambient identity + ml-pipeline principal fix -> driver->ml-pipeline hop succeeds; full run SUCCEEDED in kubeflow-admin (2026-06-25).

title: Multi-user blocked: Kubeflow aggregated ClusterRoles (kubeflow-admin/edit/view) not installed
description: Multi-user (platform-02x Option 3) verification: the api-server denies the profile owner — 'PermissionDenied: User admin@aedenjameson.com ... RBAC: clusterrole kubeflow-admin not found'. The profile-controller creates per-namespace RoleBindings in profile namespaces (kubeflow-admin) — namespaceAdmin->ClusterRole/kubeflow-admin (owner), default-editor->ClusterRole/kubeflow-edit, default-viewer->ClusterRole/kubeflow-view — but NONE of those aggregated ClusterRoles exist in the cluster. This deployment's charts (pipelines, kubeflow-dashboard, kubeflow-profiles, oauth2-proxy, istio-ingress, training-operator) never installed the upstream Kubeflow base RBAC (kubeflow-roles / common manifests) that define kubeflow-admin/kubeflow-edit/kubeflow-view. Without them every owner action via the api-server SubjectAccessReview is Unauthorized. Multi-user was never exercised before (single-user always used), so this latent gap only surfaces now. Fix: vendor/install the Kubeflow aggregated ClusterRoles (kubeflow-admin/edit/view) as a chart, OR abandon multi-user for single-user Option 2 (pipeline-runner). BLOCKS platform-02x.
acceptance_criteria: ClusterRoles kubeflow-admin/kubeflow-edit/kubeflow-view exist; profile owner can create experiments/runs in their namespace via the api-server.
notes: FIX IMPLEMENTED (code) 2026-06-24: created kubeflow/charts/kubeflow-roles chart vendoring the base aggregating ClusterRoles from kubeflow/manifests common/kubeflow-roles/base/cluster-roles.yaml — kubeflow-admin, kubeflow-edit, kubeflow-view (aggregating shells) + kubeflow-kubernetes-admin/edit/view (generic k8s member roles). Added helm_release.kubeflow_roles to kubeflow/terraform/main.tf (depends_on kubeflow ns). ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: the member roles that grant pipelines.kubeflow.org (kubeflow-pipelines-edit, labeled aggregate-to-kubeflow-edit) ALREADY exist live but were orphaned because the base kubeflow-edit/admin/view roles didn't exist to aggregate them. Once the base roles deploy, k8s auto-aggregates the existing members and the owner's namespaceAdmin->kubeflow-admin binding resolves -> grants pipelines.kubeflow.org/experiments. helm lint+template pass (6 ClusterRoles), terraform fmt clean. Needs deploy (terraform apply) then re-verify the owner can create an experiment/run.
close_reason: kubeflow-roles chart deployed; kubeflow-admin/edit/view exist and aggregate the pre-existing pipeline/istio member roles (kubeflow-admin now has 32 rules incl pipelines.kubeflow.org). VERIFIED: owner admin@aedenjameson.com can now create experiments + submit runs via the api-server (was PermissionDenied before).

title: metacontroller v4.11.22 cannot reconcile namespaces-parent CompositeController (blocks per-namespace KFP provisioning)
description: The kubeflow-pipelines-profile-controller CompositeController (composite_controller.yaml) uses parentResource {apiVersion: v1, resource: namespaces} — a CORE resource. metacontroller v4.11.22 (ghcr.io/metacontroller/metacontroller:v4.11.22) fails every reconcile with: 'CustomResourceDefinition.apiextensions.k8s.io "namespaces." not found' — it builds the CRD name as <resource>.<group> = 'namespaces.' (empty core group, trailing dot) and looks it up as a CRD, which core resources don't have. Result: the composite controller never calls its sync webhook, so it does NOT provision per-namespace KFP resources (kfp-launcher, etc.). Confirmed NOT a stale discovery cache (persists immediately after a metacontroller StatefulSet restart). Pre-existing (errors from 03:13 2026-06-24, before the multi-user rev-5 deploy at 04:19); unrelated to the platform-02x content changes. Existing kubeflow-admin children persist (metacontroller doesn't delete on failure) but new ones aren't created. This is a metacontroller v4 rewrite (controller-runtime) regression for core-resource parents that worked under metacontroller v2/v3. Fix options: (1) pin metacontroller to a v3.x that supports core parentResource; (2) upgrade to a metacontroller version where this is fixed; (3) stopgap: manually create kfp-launcher per profile namespace. BLOCKS platform-02x (multi-user; kfp-launcher needed for v2 runs in profile ns) and platform-f2i.
acceptance_criteria: The kubeflow-pipelines-profile-controller CompositeController reconciles without the 'namespaces.' CRD error; profile namespaces (e.g. kubeflow-admin) receive an auto-provisioned kfp-launcher configmap.
close_reason: RESOLVED (same root cause as platform-0vu). The metacontroller 'namespaces. not found' reconcile failure was fixed by migrating the profile-controller CompositeController->DecoratorController (metacontroller v4.x dropped core-resource CompositeController parents; KFP's own fix). Now confirmed the second half of acceptance: removed the manual stopgap kfp-launcher (label stopgap=platform-etk-manual) from kubeflow-admin and forced a DecoratorController re-sync -> the controller AUTO-PROVISIONED kfp-launcher in ~3s, now owned by the namespace (controller-managed, auto-heals), with correct content (defaultPipelineRoot=minio://mlpipeline/v2/artifacts, providers fromEnv:false + secretRef mlpipeline-minio-artifact, endpoint minio-service.kubeflow:9000). KFP_DEFAULT_PIPELINE_ROOT is set so the controller emits the kfp-launcher child. Per-namespace KFP provisioning is fully working.

title: Codify kubeflow ambient dataplane label in IaC (prevent terraform revert)
description: PoC fixed pipelines live by relabeling kubeflow ns istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient and removing istio-injection=enabled. This is NOT in IaC: kubeflow/terraform/main.tf:9 still sets istio-injection=enabled, so the next terraform apply will revert the label and re-break the pipelines UI (and ambient enrollment). Codify: (1) main.tf:9 -> istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient, drop istio-injection; (2) configure kubeflow profile-controller to label profile namespaces (kubeflow-admin + future) dataplane-mode=ambient; (3) merge the ml-pipeline-ui oauth2-proxy-principal policy fix (branch fix-pipelines-permission) to main and deploy. Verify pipelines still load after a terraform apply.
acceptance_criteria: terraform apply leaves kubeflow (and profile) namespaces labeled dataplane-mode=ambient; pipelines UI loads with the restrictive ml-pipeline-ui policy; no manual kubectl label needed.
notes: SCOPE CORRECTION 2026-06-22: reverted the profiles configmaps.yaml change that added dataplane-mode=ambient to profile namespaces. Reason: kubeflow-admin runs SIDECAR mode (native istio-proxy) and its ns-owner-access-istio L7 owner-isolation rules are enforced by those sidecars; moving profile namespaces to ambient without a waypoint (platform-xd4, deferred) would silently drop those L7 rules. Adopted a DELIBERATE per-purpose dataplane split (see bd memory kubeflow-dataplane-split): kubeflow=ambient, profile namespaces+ingress=sidecar, kubeflow-system=out-of-mesh. This issue now = ONLY codify kubeflow ns ambient (terraform main.tf, done) + merge the ml-pipeline-ui policy fix. Added an explanatory comment in configmaps.yaml warning not to re-add the ambient label until xd4 ships waypoints.
close_reason: COMPLETE & verified. Ambient dataplane labels are codified in IaC and live state matches (zero drift): (1) kubeflow ns -> terraform/main.tf:19 istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient, no istio-injection (with explanatory comment); (2) profile namespaces -> kubeflow-profiles profiles-namespace-labels-data configmap sets dataplane-mode=ambient + use-waypoint=waypoint, no istio-injection; the profile-controller setNamespaceLabels is additive so it won't re-add istio-injection. Live: kubeflow + kubeflow-admin both dataplane=ambient, injection empty. ml-pipeline-ui restrictive policy (oauth2-proxy principal, not allow-all) is in the chart and the pipelines UI loads (verified extensively this session). Fresh installs get correct labels via the admin-namespace.yaml pre-install hook; no manual kubectl label needed (the one-time istio-injection- removal this session was legacy-state cleanup). NOTE: the bead's original 2026-06-22/23 'dataplane split' notes were superseded by the xd4 waypoint work — profile namespaces now run ambient+waypoint, which is what's codified.

title: Fix ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy: allow oauth2-proxy SA, not ingress gateway
description: Pipelines UI returns Istio 'RBAC: access denied'. The ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy (authorization_policies.yaml:54-57) only allows cluster.local/ns/ingress/sa/istio-ingressgateway, but oauth2-proxy is an in-path reverse proxy (reverse_proxy=true) that upstreams /pipeline/ directly to ml-pipeline-ui (oauth2-proxy configmap.yaml:44). The real direct caller is cluster.local/ns/kubeflow/sa/oauth2-proxy. The wildcard-host ml-pipeline-ui VirtualService never wins over oauth2-proxy's exact dashboard_host VS. Fix: restrict source principals to the oauth2-proxy SA (mirrors the working kubeflow-dashboard policy) and correct the misleading comment. The /pipeline-straight-from-gateway assumption in the comment is wrong.
acceptance_criteria: ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy allows cluster.local/ns/<kubeflow-ns>/sa/oauth2-proxy; pipelines UI loads without 'RBAC: access denied'.
notes: PoC SUCCESS 2026-06-22 on dev: relabeled kubeflow ns istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient (removed istio-injection=enabled), rollout-restarted oauth2-proxy/ml-pipeline-ui/ml-pipeline. Restored ml-pipeline-ui policy to restrictive principal cluster.local/ns/kubeflow/sa/oauth2-proxy. In-cluster test: caller SA=oauth2-proxy -> HTTP 200 (allowed); caller SA=default -> connection reset/curl 56 (denied). Confirms clean ambient enrollment fixes L4 principal matching AND the restrictive policy is properly enforced (allow-all regression resolved). CAVEAT: live ns-label change is NOT in IaC — kubeflow/terraform/main.tf:9 still sets istio-injection=enabled, so next terraform apply reverts it. Must codify: (1) terraform main.tf:9 -> dataplane-mode=ambient; (2) profile-controller to label profile namespaces ambient; (3) the oauth2-proxy-principal policy fix (committed on branch fix-pipelines-permission). Still TODO for full ambient: other kubeflow namespaces, waypoints for L7 policies (ml-pipeline x-auth-request-email, profile ns-owner-access-istio), Gateway API CRDs, profile-controller gateway SA fix (platform-7d1).
close_reason: DONE & verified (chart + live). ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy is the restrictive oauth2-proxy-SA policy: chart authorization_policies.yaml -> action ALLOW, principal cluster.local/ns/{{ .Values.kubeflow.namespace }}/sa/oauth2-proxy, with corrected comment (oauth2-proxy reverse-proxies /pipeline/, not straight-from-gateway). Live deployed policy matches: principals=[cluster.local/ns/kubeflow/sa/oauth2-proxy], selector app=ml-pipeline-ui, no allow-all rule (no regression). Pipelines UI loads without 'RBAC: access denied' — verified end-to-end this session. Codification caveat handled by platform-iyr (closed); blocked platform-nkx already closed.

title: Fix ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy: allow ingress gateway SA (was oauth2-proxy)
description: Pipelines tab showed 'RBAC: access denied'. Root cause: earlier this session (platform-jk3) the ml-pipeline-ui Istio AuthorizationPolicy was restricted to sa/oauth2-proxy, but the /pipeline route is sent directly from the ingress gateway to ml-pipeline-ui (ml-pipeline-ui VirtualService), so the real caller is cluster.local/ns/ingress/sa/istio-ingressgateway. ztunnel (ambient mesh) enforced the policy and denied the gateway. Fixed: live-patched the policy + updated kubeflow/charts/pipelines (values ingress.sa=istio-ingressgateway; authorization_policies.yaml principal). Verified gateway->/pipeline returns 200. Vindicates the original reviewer suggestion (gateway SA), which platform-jk3 wrongly overrode with oauth2-proxy.

Made permanent in kubeflow/charts/pipelines: values.yaml ingress.sa=istio-ingressgateway; authorization_policies.yaml ml-pipeline-ui principal uses {{ .Values.ingress.namespace }}/sa/{{ .Values.ingress.sa }}. helm template matches live value; helm lint clean. title: Restore source-principal restriction on ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy
description: Security review finding (MEDIUM). kubeflow/charts/pipelines/templates/authorization_policies.yaml:50-54 changed the ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy rules from a principal restriction (only istio-ingressgateway SA) to allow-all (- {}). ml-pipeline-ui runs with ENABLE_AUTHZ=false and holds MinIO artifact creds in env, so the Istio policy is the only control on who can reach it. Allow-all lets any in-mesh workload reach the UI proxy directly (lateral movement / artifact disclosure). main has the secure form; the weakening is uncommitted on update-operator. Fix: revert rules to the from/source/principals block restricting to cluster.local/ns/<ingress-ns>/sa/<ingress-sa>.
acceptance_criteria: ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy rules restrict source principals to the istio-ingressgateway SA; no '- {}' allow-all remains on that policy.

title: Local helm charts not redeployed on template change
description: Root cause found during platform-9kd validation: the terraform hashicorp/helm provider keys a local-chart helm_release on chart VERSION + values, not on template file contents. PR #204's ztunnel fix merged but istio-monitoring stayed on revision 1 (never upgraded), so the live PodMonitor lacked the drop rule. Fix: feed a sha1 of each local chart dir in via values so any file change forces an upgrade. Covers istio-monitoring + lb-controller-monitor.
notes: Done + validated: #206 forced istio-monitoring to revision 2 via the chart checksum; the ztunnel drop rule reached the live PodMonitor and Prometheus config. Confirms local-chart edits now redeploy.

title: Remove monitoring namespace import block after first apply
description:
The platform-msg PR added a config-driven import {} block in prometheus/terraform/main.tf to adopt the pre-existing (Helm-created) monitoring namespace into Terraform state on existing clusters. After the first successful apply (make deploy-prometheus) on dev, remove the import block.

Why remove it: once the namespace is in state the block is a no-op, BUT it would FAIL a fresh cluster provision (terraform errors importing a non-existent object), so it must not stay in the tree long-term.

Action: delete the import block (kubernetes_namespace_v1.monitoring) and its MIGRATION comment from prometheus/terraform/main.tf; the resource block itself stays. Verify a plan shows no namespace changes afterward.
notes: Done: import block removed in #204, deployed. Namespace stays adopted (validated: revision history + ns intact).

title: Upgrade Istio control plane 1.27.0 -> 1.30.x (prereq for Gateway API v1.5.x)
description: Upgrade Istio control plane from 1.27.0 to 1.30.x. Istio pins sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api per minor: 1.27->v1.3.0, 1.28->v1.4.1, 1.30->v1.5.1 (verified from istio release-branch go.mod). The Gateway API v1.5.x CRD bump (platform-d71) requires Istio 1.30 because istiod only consumes the Gateway API fields it is compiled against; pushing CRDs ahead of the control plane gains no waypoint/L7 features and risks new v1.4/v1.5 CEL validations rejecting existing resources.
acceptance_criteria: Istio control plane on 1.30.x and healthy; gateways/HTTPRoutes/waypoints reconcile; v1.5.1 standard bundle vendored as part of upgrade.
notes:
Observability coupling (from platform-msg, the Grafana<->Istio mesh wiring). When upgrading 1.27->1.30, also update the assets in prometheus/charts/istio-monitoring, kept in lockstep with istio_repo_version at istio/system/terraform/main.tf:3:

1. Re-vendor the Istio Grafana dashboards to the TARGET release branch. Re-download mesh/service/workload/pilot(control-plane)/performance/ztunnel from istio/istio release-1.30 manifests/addons/dashboards into prometheus/charts/istio-monitoring/dashboards/. Dashboard PromQL tracks metric/label changes across minors, so stale 1.27 dashboards will show gaps/empty panels on 1.30.

2. Re-verify scrape config port names/paths against the 1.30 charts (version-checked, not guaranteed stable across minors):
   - servicemonitor-istiod.yaml: istiod Service port http-monitoring (15014)
   - podmonitor-ztunnel.yaml: ztunnel pod port name ztunnel-stats (15020), path /metrics (ztunnel is Rust, not Envoy)
   - podmonitor-envoy.yaml: relies on meshConfig.enablePrometheusMerge=true and /stats/prometheus on istio-proxy containers

3. Do the dashboard re-vendor and port re-verify in the SAME change as the control-plane bump so metrics do not silently go empty mid-upgrade. Stepwise 1.27->1.28->1.29->1.30, not a direct jump.
Dashboard re-vendor on the Istio upgrade now auto-redeploys: prometheus/terraform/main.tf feeds a chart-content checksum via values, so editing chart files (e.g. swapping dashboard JSON) forces a helm upgrade without a Chart.yaml version bump.

title: KFP multi-user 'Teams': contributor-aware per-profile waypoint isolation (multi-member profiles)
description:
GitHub issue #157 ("Explore KFP Multi-User Auth and Teams") is largely satisfied: KFP runs in MULTIUSER mode (X-Forwarded-Email userid header via oauth2-proxy), and platform-lyl shipped per-profile-namespace automation — the kubeflow-profiles chart (1.0.8) now loops local.profiles, so adding an OWNER-ONLY profile is just appending {namespace, owner} to terraform local.profiles and redeploying (waypoint Gateway, ns-owner-access-waypoint, ml-pipeline default-editor principal all auto-provisioned).

What remains for the "Teams" half of #157: a profile today is single-OWNER only. The L7 owner-isolation enforced at the waypoint (kubeflow-profiles/templates/ns-owner-access-waypoint.yaml) allows exactly one email — the profile owner — in its `when: request.headers[x-forwarded-email]` values. Real teams add CONTRIBUTORS via KFAM (the central dashboard "Manage Contributors" flow, already wired: PROFILES_KFAM_SERVICE_HOST). KFAM creates namespace-wide RoleBindings + AuthorizationPolicies, but in ambient mode those attach to ztunnel and their L7 rules are silently dropped (the same root cause platform-lyl fixed for the owner). Net effect: contributor access to a team namespace is NOT L7-enforced at the waypoint, so multi-member teams either can't reach their namespace's KFP UI/artifacts or bypass isolation.

Work: make the per-profile waypoint policy contributor-aware so a profile can have multiple members with correct L7 isolation. Options:
- add profiles[].contributors to local.profiles and template their emails into ns-owner-access-waypoint values (static, simplest); or
- reconcile KFAM-created per-contributor RoleBindings/AuthorizationPolicies into waypoint-targeted (spec.targetRefs -> waypoint Gateway) policies so the dashboard "Manage Contributors" flow works dynamically (preferred for true self-service teams).

Also close out the verification loop platform-lyl left open: a full end-to-end pipeline RUN by a non-owner member (not just the driver->ml-pipeline hop) was never literally executed.

Files: kubeflow/charts/kubeflow-profiles/templates/ns-owner-access-waypoint.yaml, waypoint.yaml, values.yaml; kubeflow/terraform/main.tf (local.profiles); kubeflow/charts/pipelines/templates/authorization_policies.yaml (profile_namespaces principals already covered).
acceptance_criteria: A profile with >1 member: the owner AND each contributor can reach that namespace's KFP UI/artifacts through the waypoint (L7 ALLOW on x-forwarded-email), while a non-member is DENIED; a contributor (non-owner) can run a pipeline in the namespace to completion (driver reaches ml-pipeline and the run Succeeds). Adding/removing a contributor via the dashboard 'Manage Contributors' flow updates enforcement without manual policy edits (or, if static, documents the local.profiles[].contributors workflow).

title: Dynamic per-profile-namespace waypoint + ml-pipeline principal automation
description: platform-xd4 templates a waypoint Gateway only for the ADMIN profile namespace (kubeflow-profiles/templates/waypoint.yaml) and enumerates only kubeflow-admin's default-editor in the ml-pipeline AuthorizationPolicy (.Values.profile_namespaces). But the profile-controller's namespace-labels (profiles-namespace-labels-data) apply istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient + istio.io/use-waypoint=waypoint to ALL profile namespaces. Consequence for any NON-admin profile: (1) it gets the use-waypoint label but NO matching waypoint Gateway -> its L7 ns-owner-access-istio owner isolation is NOT enforced (silent security gap); (2) its default-editor SA is not in the ml-pipeline policy principals -> KFP runs in that namespace fail at the driver->ml-pipeline hop (same as platform-nhv). Need automation that, per provisioned profile namespace, creates a 'waypoint' Gateway AND adds cluster.local/ns/<profile-ns>/sa/default-editor (exact; ztunnel has no wildcard) to the ml-pipeline + (eventually) metadata/minio policies. Options: extend the profile-controller (it currently only applies labels), a small sidecar controller, or templating a known-profiles list across the profiles + pipelines charts. Until then, multi-user is validated for the admin profile only. Surfaced via PR review of platform-xd4.
acceptance_criteria: Creating a new Profile yields a working multi-user namespace: a waypoint Gateway exists in it (ns-owner-access-istio L7 enforced, owner allowed / non-owner denied), and a no-SA pipeline run by that owner Succeeds (driver reaches ml-pipeline).
notes: VERIFIED LIVE with a throwaway test profile (kubeflow-test, owner test-owner@aedenjameson.com). Rendered the refactored profiles chart with the test profile + the pipelines policies with profile_namespaces=[kubeflow-admin,kubeflow-test], applied them, and confirmed the new profile came up FULLY automatically: default-editor SA + owner RoleBinding (namespaceAdmin) + ns-owner-access-istio (profile-controller); kfp-launcher + ml-pipeline-ui-artifact (DecoratorController); waypoint Gateway Accepted=True Programmed=True; ns-owner-access-waypoint Accepted (L7 enforced at the waypoint); kubeflow-test/sa/default-editor added to the ml-pipeline/minio/metadata-grpc policies. title: Deploy waypoint proxies + Gateway API CRDs for L7 Kubeflow AuthorizationPolicies
description: In ambient mode ztunnel silently drops L7 rules (request.headers[x-auth-request-email], paths, methods). Pipelines work without waypoints because their effective rules are L4, but notebooks/KServe rely on the profile-controller ns-owner-access-istio policy whose L7 rules are dropped -> access will break. Also ml-pipeline's x-auth-request-email rule is dropped (currently masked by its L4 principal rule). Install Gateway API CRDs (currently absent), deploy waypoint proxies for the namespaces/workloads that need L7 authz (ml-pipeline, profile namespaces), and bind with istio.io/use-waypoint. Depends on the ambient labeling work.
acceptance_criteria: Gateway API CRDs installed; waypoints enforce L7 rules for ml-pipeline and profile ns-owner-access-istio; notebooks load for the namespace owner and are denied for non-owners.
notes: NON-OWNER TEST 2026-06-25 (headless, via ml-pipeline api): owner admin@aedenjameson.com -> HTTP 200 listing kubeflow-admin pipelines; non-owner intruder@example.com -> HTTP 403 code 7 'PermissionDenied: User intruder@example.com is not authorized' for BOTH pipelines and runs. Proves KFP multi-user owner-isolation (api-server checks email vs the profile's KFAM RoleBindings). NOTE: this is the KFP-RBAC layer; the Istio WAYPOINT L7 owner-isolation (ns-owner-access-waypoint, for profile-namespace services like notebooks) is structurally verified (status Accepted + lowercase request.headers[x-forwarded-email] when-clause) but NOT behaviorally tested for non-owner deny because no notebook/profile service is deployed and injecting a non-owner email through the waypoint needs a 2nd OIDC user. Pipelines owner-isolation: DONE & proven.
close_reason: Delivered & verified: Gateway API CRDs installed; istio-waypoint GatewayClass + waypoint Gateway for kubeflow-admin; ns-owner-access-waypoint AuthorizationPolicy enforced AT THE WAYPOINT (status Accepted, lowercase request.headers[x-forwarded-email] when-clause) — fixes the ztunnel L7-drop gap. Owner-isolation proven at the KFP layer: owner HTTP 200, non-owner intruder@example.com HTTP 403 PermissionDenied on kubeflow-admin pipelines+runs. No-SA multi-user pipeline runs succeed. RESIDUAL (tracked, non-blocking): behavioral non-owner deny at the waypoint for profile-namespace SERVICES (notebooks) untested — no notebook deployed and needs a 2nd OIDC user; enforcement is structurally in place. title: Decide ServiceAccount strategy for single-user pipeline runs (default-editor in kubeflow ns)
description:
KFP 2.16.1 api-server runs in single-user mode (MULTIUSER=false), so pipeline runs execute in the 'kubeflow' namespace. The api-server default pipeline-runner SA is 'default-editor' (DEFAULTPIPELINERUNNERSERVICEACCOUNT in pipeline-api-server-config), but 'default-editor' only exists in profile namespaces (e.g. kubeflow-admin), NOT in 'kubeflow'. As a result, default pipeline runs in 'kubeflow' fail with: pods forbidden: serviceaccount "default-editor" not found.

During the 2.16.1 upgrade E2E verification (bead platform-kin), the sample pipeline only succeeded by overriding service_account=pipeline-runner at submit time. That is a workaround, not a default.

Decision needed - pick one:
1. Provision a 'default-editor' SA (+ appropriate edit RBAC / binding) in the 'kubeflow' namespace so default single-user runs work out of the box.
2. Change DEFAULTPIPELINERUNNERSERVICEACCOUNT to 'pipeline-runner' (already exists in 'kubeflow' with the pipeline-runner role) so the api-server default works without per-run overrides.
3. Move to true multi-user (run pipelines in profile namespaces like kubeflow-admin that already have default-editor + mlpipeline-minio-artifact), adjusting api-server MULTIUSER and routing accordingly.

Acceptance: a pipeline submitted with no explicit service_account runs to completion in the default namespace.
notes: ARCHITECTURE CONCLUSION 2026-06-24: multi-user (Option 3) is gated on a dataplane change, not just policy tweaks. Verified working so far: ClusterRoles/owner-authz (38t closed), no-SA routing to kubeflow-admin, default-editor as runner SA, kfp-launcher (stopgap). The run FAILS at the driver->ml-pipeline gRPC hop because KFP step pods (inject=false) in SIDECAR-mode profile namespaces have NO mesh identity, so ambient ml-pipeline's principal authz rejects them (platform-nhv root cause). FULL remaining dependency chain for Option 3: (1) flip profile namespaces to AMBIENT dataplane-mode (so istio-cni captures inject=false step pods -> identity) — reverses the kubeflow-dataplane-split decision; (2) platform-xd4 waypoints to keep L7 ns-owner-access-istio owner isolation working under ambient; (3) platform-nhv: add exact step-pod principals (ns/<profile>/sa/default-editor) to ml-pipeline/metadata-grpc/minio/mysql policies; (4) platform-etk metacontroller fix for kfp-launcher auto-provisioning. Option 2 (single-user, pipeline-runner) still avoids ALL of (1)-(4).
close_reason: VERIFIED 2026-06-25: Option 3 multi-user end-to-end — no-SA run as admin@aedenjameson.com SUCCEEDED in kubeflow-admin, all pods as default-editor. Acceptance met. Remaining generalization/security tracked: platform-etk (kfp-launcher auto-provision; manual stopgap used), platform-xd4 (owner-isolation L7 at waypoint), platform-lyl (dynamic per-profile).

notes: Sibling of platform-oaz (Postgres migration). Both defer an upstream-default backend change out of the version upgrade. Depends on platform-kin landing first.

Bead scope item 'retire vestigial TF random_password.minio_access_key/secret_key' is a non-issue: those were REPURPOSED as the SeaweedFS admin S3 creds (legacy naming retained by design). Remaining 'minio' strings are load-bearing legacy names (the mlpipeline-minio-artifact secret name is what KFP/Argo expect) — a cosmetic rename would be risky churn on the live cred path with no functional gain, so intentionally not done.

title: Migrate Kubeflow Pipelines DB backend from MySQL to PostgreSQL
description:
As part of (or following) the KFP 2.3.0 -> 2.16.1 upgrade, evaluate and migrate the pipelines chart database backend from MySQL 8.0.26 to PostgreSQL. KFP 2.16.1 has first-class PostgreSQL support (manifests/kustomize/third-party/postgresql and base/postgresql). This is intentionally scoped separately from the version upgrade to keep blast radius contained.

Scope:
- Replace the MySQL deployment/service/secret in kubeflow/charts/pipelines with a PostgreSQL deployment (or external RDS).
- Update api-server, cache-server, metadata-grpc, and persistence-agent DB config env (dbType/driver, host, port, secrets) for Postgres.
- Update pipeline-install-config and mysql-secret -> postgres equivalents.
- Decide on in-cluster Postgres vs AWS RDS PostgreSQL.
- Plan data migration / fresh-deploy strategy (terragrunt apply/destroy currently does fresh deploys).

Depends on the KFP 2.16.1 upgrade landing first (MySQL kept during that upgrade for minimal change).
notes: OUTCOME: Postgres migration REVERTED (PR #208) and MySQL restored + verified working live. Root blocker (confirmed against the local kubeflow/pipelines master checkout, not just 2.16.1): the KFP cache-server has NO Postgres support in ANY version - backend/src/cache/ contains zero postgres/pgx refs, only gorm.io/driver/mysql, switch handles only case mysql -> Fatalf. api-server + MLMD do support Postgres (driver name 'pgx' for api-server), but cache-server is a hard blocker. This is DEFERRED until upstream adds cache-server Postgres support (or a hybrid mysql-cache+postgres-rest is accepted, which doubles DB infra and was rejected). Not actionable now.
close_reason: WON'T DO — not viable on KFP, reverted. OUTCOME: attempted the in-cluster MySQL->PostgreSQL migration (PR #205, deployed). It broke KFP on the live cluster: the cache-server CrashLoopBackOff'd with 'Driver pgx is not supported'. ROOT CAUSE (verified against the local kubeflow/pipelines master checkout, not just 2.16.1): the KFP cache-server has NO PostgreSQL support in ANY version — backend/src/cache/ contains zero postgres/pgx references, imports only gorm.io/driver/mysql, the driver switch handles only case mysql -> Fatalf, and gorm.Open(mysql.Open()) is hardcoded. The api-server (driver 'pgx') and MLMD DO support Postgres, but cache-server is a hard blocker, so an all-Postgres backend is impossible on current KFP. A hybrid (Postgres for api+MLMD, MySQL for cache) was rejected — it doubles DB infra and defeats the consolidation goal. RESOLUTION: reverted to MySQL (PR #208), restored service, and additionally hardened the MySQL root password (PR #209, bd platform-3mr). VERIFIED END-TO-END live on archegos-dev-eks: mysql + cache-server + metadata-grpc + ml-pipeline all 1/1 Running, clients auth with the generated password (no errors), and a test pipeline ran PENDING->RUNNING->SUCCEEDED (exercising mlpipeline/metadb/cachedb). Orphaned postgres resources cleaned up. NOT re-attemptable until upstream adds cache-server Postgres support; re-open/file a new bead if that lands.

description: infra-validate-plan Plan step set TF_VAR_kube_data_auth_enabled=true, so the aws_eks_cluster data source in the generated kube/helm provider tried to read the mocked cluster (mock-cluster-name) and failed with 'couldn't find resource'. make plan-all works because plan-all sets false. Changed CI Plan step to false to match (apply/destroy keep true).
close_reason: Set TF_VAR_kube_data_auth_enabled=false on the infra-validate-plan Plan step (was true) so the aws_eks_cluster data source stays gated off during greenfield plan, matching make plan-all. apply/destroy keep true (cluster exists then). YAML valid.

title: CI: run Terraform (not OpenTofu) in infra workflows
description: infra-validate-plan/apply/destroy pinned tofu_version=1.9.8, but 1.9.8 is a Terraform version; OpenTofu has no 1.9.8 so mise 404'd. gruntwork-io/terragrunt-action has no tf_version input (only tofu_version/tf_path). Added hashicorp/setup-terraform (1.9.8) install step + tf_path: terraform on each terragrunt-action step so Terragrunt uses the terraform binary. Aligns CI with .tool-versions (terraform 1.9.8).
close_reason: All 3 workflows: added 'Install Terraform' step (hashicorp/setup-terraform@b9cd54a v3.1.2, terraform_version from env tf_version=1.9.8, wrapper off) after AWS creds; replaced tofu_version/tf_version action inputs with tf_path: terraform (satisfies action.yml mise-check and makes Terragrunt use the terraform binary; default tfpath is terraform anyway). env renamed tofu_version->tf_version. .tool-versions unchanged (still terraform 1.9.8). YAML validated. Not committed.

title: Centralize Route53 zone lookup in account module; pass root_zone_id to consumers
description: Option 2 from plan discussion: dex/prometheus/kiali/kubeflow each ran a live data.aws_route53_zone lookup keyed on root_domain, which fails during run-all plan on greenfield because account is mocked to mock.example.com. Move the lookup into the account module, output root_zone_id, and have consumers take zone_id from dependency.account.outputs.root_zone_id (mockable). Removes live route53 calls from 4 consumer plans.
close_reason: Moved route53 zone lookup into account module (data.aws_route53_zone.root + output root_zone_id); added mock root_zone_id; rewired dex/prometheus/kiali/kubeflow to consume var.root_zone_id from dependency.account.outputs.root_zone_id instead of their own live lookup. make plan-all now plans all modules cleanly: account does the single live lookup (Z0873790MAHSTW1NBQTC), consumers plan with mock zone_id (acm validation record known-after-apply). terraform fmt + terragrunt hclfmt clean.

title: Gate kubernetes/helm provider exec auth on kube_data_auth_enabled
description: Reviewer: exec (aws eks get-token) was always configured even when kube_data_auth_enabled=false, so the plan path (make plan-all, flag=false) forced get-token on refresh instead of falling back to kubeconfig. Wrapped exec in dynamic block gated on the flag in common/kube-provider.hcl + common/helm-provider.hcl; apply (flag=true via Makefile) keeps the exec/token-expiry fix. Fixed stale variable description (named removed aws_eks_cluster_auth data source) across 12 variables.tf.
close_reason: Wrapped exec in dynamic block gated on var.kube_data_auth_enabled in common/kube-provider.hcl and common/helm-provider.hcl (apply/flag=true keeps exec token-expiry fix; plan/flag=false drops exec -> kubeconfig fallback). Updated stale description naming the removed aws_eks_cluster_auth data source across all 12 variables.tf. terragrunt hclfmt clean; no aws_eks_cluster_auth references remain.

title: Fix misleading inbound-cidrs=0.0.0.0/0 comment on admin ALB ingresses
description: Security review flagged prometheus/terraform/main.tf:94 — the comment implies 0.0.0.0/0 is recommended, when it means the ALB is open to the entire internet. Replace/add an accurate comment across all four admin ingresses (grafana, kiali, dex, dashboard) that share one ALB group. Comment-only; value unchanged.
close_reason: Replaced misleading inbound-cidrs=0.0.0.0/0 comment (grafana, kiali) and added matching accurate comment (dex, dashboard) across all four shared-ALB admin ingresses. Comment-only; value unchanged. terraform fmt clean on all four modules.

title: Audit and tighten remaining allow-all (- {}) Istio AuthorizationPolicies in pipelines chart
description: kubeflow/charts/pipelines/templates/authorization_policies.yaml has additional allow-all rules (- {}): line 11-14, minio (line 35-38), and cache-server (line 142-145). These accept any in-mesh source. Likely pre-existing (separate from the ml-pipeline-ui finding in platform-nkx). Review each: determine the legitimate callers and replace - {} with from/source/principals restrictions where feasible, or document why allow-all is required. minio is highest concern since it serves artifacts with credentials.
acceptance_criteria: Each remaining '- {}' policy (line 11, minio, cache-server) is either scoped to specific source principals or has a documented justification for allow-all.
notes: DONE 2026-06-23 (documentation approach). Added ambient-aware justification comments to all 3 allow-all rules in pipelines/authorization_policies.yaml: (1) service-cache-server = PERMANENT allow-all (caller is cache-webhook-kubeflow mutating webhook -> kube-apiserver, out of mesh, no SPIFFE identity; cannot be scoped). (2) metadata-grpc-service = justified for now; callers (metadata-envoy sa=default, metadata-writer, ml-pipeline, step pods) are mostly ambient-captured but scoping blocked by step-pod SA decision + external gateway /ml_metadata path. (3) minio-service = justified for now; catch-all exists for artifact-writing step pods whose SA = open platform-02x decision; highest-value to scope but unsafe until 02x resolved + live run. Re-verified a0v's premise is now PARTIALLY FALSE under ambient (callers DO get identities except kube-apiserver). Actual scoping tracked in new issue (blocked on platform-02x + platform-iyr). helm template renders; comments only, no behavior change.
close_reason: All 3 allow-all rules documented with ambient-aware justifications in pipelines/authorization_policies.yaml: service-cache-server permanently required (kube-apiserver webhook, out of mesh); minio-service + metadata-grpc-service justified now, actual scoping deferred to platform-f2i (blocked on platform-02x SA decision + platform-iyr ambient landing). Acceptance met (each allow-all scoped or documented).

title: Harden KFP MySQL root password (drop MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD)
description: The KFP pipelines mysql backend uses the upstream default of an empty root password: mysql-secret sets password to empty and the mysql Deployment sets MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD=true. DB auth is then effectively open (root, no password), protected only by the Istio AuthorizationPolicy that scopes callers to specific SAs. Add defense-in-depth: wire a terraform random_password into mysql-secret (password) + the mysql container MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD env, and remove MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD. The DB clients already read DBCONFIG_PASSWORD/mysql_config_password from mysql-secret, so it flows through. Mirrors the postgres_password pattern. Surfaced by Copilot on the revert PR #208 (pre-existing upstream default, not introduced by the revert). Note: MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD only applies on fresh datadir init - fine since mysql re-deploys fresh after the postgres revert.
acceptance_criteria: mysql-secret password sourced from terraform random_password; mysql container uses MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD and no longer sets MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD; KFP DB clients connect with the password; pipeline runs succeed.
close_reason: Deployed + verified live on archegos-dev-eks (PR #209, chart 1.4.2, release rev 20). mysql now uses a terraform-generated MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD (mysql-secret password non-empty, b64 len 44 = 32 bytes), MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD removed, PVC cut v2->v3 forcing a fresh datadir so the password applies on first bootstrap. Followed up by restarting the DB-client deployments (ml-pipeline/cache-server/metadata-grpc) so they re-read the rotated secret; they reconnected with no auth errors (ml-pipeline: 'Client manager initialized successfully' + 'Starting RPC server'; cache-server Running 0 restarts). NOTE the v3 cutover wiped prior KFP run history (expected, fresh-deploy model). Stacked-PR gotcha: #209 merged into the revert branch not main; re-landed via a direct merge to main.

title: envoy-stats-monitor double-scrapes ztunnel
description: Discovered during platform-msg post-deploy validation: ztunnel pods match envoy-stats-monitor (container istio-proxy + prometheus.io/port=15020), so they were scraped at /stats/prometheus in addition to ztunnel-monitor's /metrics. Confirmed ztunnel /metrics and /stats/prometheus are byte-identical, so it was pure duplication. Fix: drop app=ztunnel in podmonitor-envoy.yaml.
notes:
Code fix merged in #204 but did NOT deploy: terraform helm provider didn't upgrade the local chart (stayed revision 1). Actual deployment is unblocked by the chart-checksum fix (bead platform-mtm). Re-validate after that applies.
Done + validated on dev: after #206 deploy, envoy-stats-monitor shows 0 ztunnel pods (4 legit Envoy targets), ztunnel-monitor still 6/6.

title: Fix stale cluster_version mock in common/mocks.hcl (1.31 -> 1.33)
description: common/mocks.hcl:20 sets the eks dependency mock cluster_version = 1.31, but the live cluster is on Kubernetes 1.33 (eks/cluster/terragrunt.hcl:59). This mock_outputs value is used by terragrunt when the real eks/cluster output is unavailable (isolated terragrunt plan/validate, e.g. the Infra - Validate & Plan CI job). The CSI addon modules (efs-csi/ebs-csi/fsx-csi) consume dependency.eks.outputs.cluster_version -> kubernetes_version, so a stale mock resolves CSI driver addon versions against the wrong k8s version during mock-only plans, producing misleading plan output. Update the mock to 1.33 to match the deployed cluster. Low impact (mock only, no effect on real applies) but a latent inconsistency. Surfaced while scoping platform-zxc (EKS upgrade) - keep this mock in sync with the cluster version on every future EKS bump.
acceptance_criteria: common/mocks.hcl cluster_version = 1.33 (matches eks/cluster/terragrunt.hcl); terragrunt plan/validate on CSI addon modules mocks against the correct k8s version.
close_reason: Fixed via PR #201 (merged). common/mocks.hcl eks mock cluster_version bumped 1.31 -> 1.33 on main, now matching eks/cluster/terragrunt.hcl kubernetes_version=1.33. Mock-only change (affects terragrunt plan/validate mock_outputs when the real eks output is unavailable, e.g. the Infra Validate & Plan CI job feeding the CSI addon modules' kubernetes_version); no runtime resources, so no live-cluster deploy/verification applicable. Consistency confirmed on origin/main.

title: Remove stale sidecar.istio.io/inject annotation from oauth2-proxy deployment
description: kubeflow/charts/oauth2-proxy/templates/deployment.yaml sets sidecar.istio.io/inject: true, but the kubeflow namespace is ambient (istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient) so no sidecar is injected (pod has a single container). The annotation is inert and misleading, and a latent footgun: if ambient were ever disabled, a sidecar would inject and the connection peer would become loopback, changing X-Forwarded-* handling. Remove the annotation so the chart honestly reflects ambient. (Surfaced in PR #196 review.)
acceptance_criteria: inject annotation removed from oauth2-proxy deployment.yaml; pod still runs in ambient; login flow unaffected
close_reason: Removed stale sidecar.istio.io/inject annotation from oauth2-proxy deployment (PR #198 merged+deployed). Verified live on archegos-dev-eks: pod rolled (new pod oauth2-proxy-5b97f4ddd8-6vcdf), single container, 1/1 Running 0 restarts; running pod has no inject annotation (inject=[]); still ambient-captured + authz-enforced (new pod served an ingress-gateway-originated request gated by the ingress-gateway-principal AuthorizationPolicy, which is a ztunnel/ambient L4 control -> proves capture); login flow intact (302 -> Dex authorize) and real client IP still honored (logged 97.126.161.20). checksum/config annotation retained.

title: Harden oauth2-proxy with trusted-proxy-ip (X-Forwarded-* trust)
description: v7.15.3 logs a startup WARNING: --reverse-proxy is enabled but no --trusted-proxy-ip CIDRs configured, so all connecting IPs (0.0.0.0/0, ::/0) are trusted to supply X-Forwarded-* headers. This is the backwards-compatible default (auth works), but v7.15.2 introduced --trusted-proxy-ip as defense-in-depth against X-Forwarded-* spoofing. Restrict trust by setting the trusted_proxy_ips option in kubeflow/charts/oauth2-proxy/templates/configmap.yaml to the VPC CIDR (gateway peer under ambient) plus loopback. Surfaced during the v7.6->v7.15.3 upgrade (platform-qk9).
acceptance_criteria: trusted_proxy_ips configured (VPC CIDR + loopback); startup warning gone; login flow + real client IP logging still work
close_reason: Hardened oauth2-proxy X-Forwarded-* trust (PR #196 merged+deployed). Set trusted_proxy_ips=[VPC CIDR, 127.0.0.0/8] via var.vpc_cidr_block; loopback added per review for sidecar-injection robustness. Verified live on archegos-dev-eks: running configmap shows trusted_proxy_ips=["10.0.0.0/16","127.0.0.0/8"]; pod rolled (config checksum) 1/1 Running 0 restarts; startup --trusted-proxy-ip WARNING gone; auth flow intact (unauth request -> 302 Dex); real-client-IP STILL honored (marked probe logged client IP 97.126.161.20 == my egress, not gateway/loopback -> XFF correctly trusted from gateway peer in VPC CIDR). Determined peer empirically via ztunnel access logs (src.addr=ingress-gateway pod IP under ambient). Follow-up platform-5wb filed to remove the stale inject annotation.

title: Connect Grafana/Prometheus to the Istio mesh for observability
description:
Grafana is installed (kube-prometheus-stack v76.4.0, prometheus/terraform/main.tf), but it has effectively zero Istio integration, so the 'properly connected to Istio' requirement of gh-71 is unmet. Mesh telemetry is neither scraped nor visualized. Scope (the three gaps):

1. Mesh metrics scraping — add a PodMonitor/ServiceMonitor for istiod (istio-system) and for Envoy sidecars (port 15090, all namespaces) so Prometheus collects mesh metrics. Today the only ServiceMonitor in the repo is the AWS LB controller one (prometheus/charts/lb-controller-monitor/templates/servicemonitor.yaml).

2. Istio dashboards — bundle the upstream Istio Grafana dashboards (mesh / service / workload / control-plane / performance) as ConfigMaps labeled grafana_dashboard=1 so the kube-prometheus-stack Grafana sidecar auto-loads them. None exist today.

3. Monitoring namespace mesh posture — the monitoring namespace is created implicitly via Helm create_namespace=true (prometheus/terraform/main.tf:10) with no explicit kubernetes_namespace resource, no istio-injection/ambient label, and no PeerAuthentication/AuthorizationPolicy. Decide whether to bring Grafana into the ambient mesh (mTLS) and, if so, add the namespace label + STRICT PeerAuthentication. Note: many shops leave Prometheus itself outside the mesh due to init-container ordering pain; injecting only Grafana is a reasonable middle ground.

Context: assessed 2026-06-26 against gh-71 and its 2026-05-26 review comment; Grafana-the-install is done, this bead tracks the remaining mesh-observability wiring.
acceptance_criteria: istiod + Envoy (15090) metrics visible in Prometheus targets; Istio mesh/service/workload/control-plane/performance dashboards present in Grafana and populated; monitoring namespace mesh posture decided and applied (label + PeerAuthentication if injected, or explicitly documented as out-of-mesh).
notes:
Split out from gh-71 (Install Grafana): install is complete, mesh integration is not. Kiali already points at Prometheus/Grafana (istio/kiali/terraform/main.tf) but dashboards/metrics are empty without items 1-2.
Deployed to dev (#203) + post-deploy fixes (#204 ztunnel/import-block, #206 chart-redeploy). Validated in-cluster: istiod 1/1, ztunnel 6/6, envoy-stats 4/4 (no ztunnel dup), 6 dashboard ConfigMaps loaded, namespace out-of-mesh. Recommend a visual spot-check of Grafana Istio dashboards + Kiali graph before final close.

title: Add CI helm lint/template check for kubeflow charts
description: Chart changes (image bumps, value edits) currently rely on manual verification; there is no automated check that the kubeflow/charts/* templates still render. A broken template or bad value would only surface at deploy time. Add a CI job that runs helm lint + helm template (and ideally kubeconform/kubeval on the rendered output) across the charts under kubeflow/charts/.
acceptance_criteria: A CI workflow (GitHub Actions) runs on PRs touching kubeflow/charts/** that executes helm lint and helm template for each chart and fails on render errors; optionally validates rendered manifests against the cluster's Kubernetes API schema.
notes: Motivated by PR #188 (training-operator + profile-controller image bumps) where rendering was only checked by hand. Charts: pipelines, training-operator, kubeflow-dashboard, oauth2-proxy, istio-ingress, kubeflow-issuer. Part of GitHub issue #158 component-currency work.
close_reason: CI helm lint/template/kubeconform workflow for kubeflow charts merged via PR #200. Workflow self-validated green on its own PR (Charts - Helm Validate: success) and proven to fail on invalid manifests (kubeconform exit 1). Includes Copilot-review hardening: --include-crds (renders the 20 CRDs in pipelines/training-operator/kubeflow-profiles), -kubernetes-version 1.33.0 (deterministic, cluster-aligned), pv-fsx ci-values fixture. Now active on future PRs touching kubeflow/charts/**.

title: Migrate Kubeflow Training Operator v1 -> Kubeflow Trainer v2.2.1
description: Follow-up to platform-rp2 (which took Option A: v1.9.3 patch bump). Kubeflow rebranded the training-operator to 'Kubeflow Trainer' (repo kubeflow/trainer); latest is v2.2.1. v2 is NOT a tag bump: it replaces the per-framework v1 CRDs (TFJob/PyTorchJob/MPIJob/XGBoostJob/PaddleJob/JAXJob, group kubeflow.org) with new TrainJob/TrainingRuntime CRDs under API group trainer.kubeflow.org (KEP-2170).
acceptance_criteria: Migration plan filed and reviewed; Trainer v2.2.1 control plane deployed; example consumers migrated from PyTorchJob to TrainJob/TrainingRuntime; v1 CRDs/operator decommissioned or run side-by-side per plan.
notes: Blast radius: kubeflow/charts/training-operator chart (deployment + 6 v1 CRDs) and the PyTorchJob example notebook (examples/training-operator/pytorchjobs/) which uses TrainingClient(PYTORCHJOB_KIND). Consumers must be rewritten to the v2 TrainJob model. Decide side-by-side vs hard cutover. Part of GitHub issue #158 (Update Kubeflow Components).

title: Add non-root securityContext to KFP profile-controller deployment
description: The kubeflow-pipelines-profile-controller container in kubeflow/charts/pipelines/templates/deployments.yaml runs without a securityContext. Upstream KFP 2.16.1 runs this deployment as non-root (UID 1000) with dropped Linux capabilities. We recently swapped its image from python:3.9 to docker.io/alpine/k8s:1.32.3 to match upstream, but deferred the securityContext hardening.
acceptance_criteria: profile-controller container/pod runs as non-root (runAsNonRoot: true, runAsUser: 1000) with capabilities dropped (drop: [ALL]), matching upstream KFP 2.16.1; pod still starts and sync.py reconciles profiles correctly against the existing MinIO backend.
notes: Keep our MinIO env wiring (MINIO_ACCESS_KEY/MINIO_SECRET_KEY) — do NOT adopt upstream's SeaweedFS endpoints (tracked separately in platform-wiz). Scope is securityContext only. Verify the alpine/k8s image can run /hooks/sync.py as UID 1000 (mounted ConfigMap is read-only, should be fine).
close_reason: Added non-root securityContext to KFP profile-controller, matching upstream KFP 2.16.1 + a hardening improvement from PR #199 review (runAsGroup 0 -> 1000, since upstream's GID 0 is OpenShift convention not needed on EKS). PR #199 merged+deployed. Verified live on archegos-dev-eks: new pod 1/1 Running, 0 restarts (no crashloop); effective securityContext exactly as intended (pod: runAsNonRoot+RuntimeDefault seccomp; container: runAsUser 1000, runAsGroup 1000, allowPrivilegeEscalation false, capabilities drop ALL, seccomp RuntimeDefault). Proven non-root (runAsNonRoot:true + Running = kubelet enforced). sync.py reconciles correctly as UID/GID 1000 (metacontroller POST /sync -> HTTP 200, no permission errors/tracebacks). All acceptance criteria met.

title: Update Gateway API CRDs from v1.2.1 to v1.5.x
description: The vendored gateway-api-crds chart (istio/system/charts/gateway-api-crds, appVersion 1.2.1 / bundle-version v1.2.1) lags upstream Gateway API stable v1.5.1 (released 2026). These CRDs back the waypoint proxy / L7 Kubeflow AuthorizationPolicy work (platform-xd4, platform-lyl). Before bumping, confirm the installed Istio control plane (currently 1.27.0) supports the target Gateway API version and which channel (standard vs experimental) is required by the waypoint config. Replace crds/standard-install.yaml with the v1.5.x bundle and bump Chart.yaml appVersion. CRDs must be applied with kubectl apply/replace (not pruned). Verify Gateways, HTTPRoutes, and waypoints still reconcile. ACCEPTANCE: bundle-version updated; istio + waypoints healthy on new CRDs.

comments.author: Aeden Jameson
comments.text: Compatibility gate (per issue instruction) checked: Istio pins sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api per minor — 1.27->v1.3.0, 1.28->v1.4.1, 1.30->v1.5.1 (verified from istio release-branch go.mod). Installed control plane is 1.27.0 (Gateway API v1.3.0). Target v1.5.x is Istio 1.30's version. istiod only consumes Gateway API fields it was compiled against, so vendoring v1.5 CRDs ahead of the control plane gains no waypoint/L7 features and risks v1.4/v1.5 CEL validation rejecting existing Gateways/HTTPRoutes. DECISION (2026-06-26): hold the v1.5.x bump until Istio is on 1.30. Blocked by platform-apb.

title: Standardize ingress gateway SA reference across kubeflow charts
description: Cross-chart inconsistency in how the ingress gateway service account is referenced: kubeflow-dashboard declares ingress.service_account=istio-ingressgateway in values.yaml but NO template uses it (dead key; its authz policy hardcodes sa/oauth2-proxy); oauth2-proxy hardcodes cluster.local/ns/{{ .Values.ingress.namespace }}/sa/istio-ingressgateway in its AuthorizationPolicy; pipelines (ml-pipeline-ui) now also hardcodes the same. Cleanup: pick one convention (e.g. ingress.service_account) and use it consistently across all kubeflow charts' AuthorizationPolicies, and remove the dead/unused key in kubeflow-dashboard. Surfaced during the ml-pipeline-ui authz fix (platform-aev).
notes: PR feedback follow-up 2026-06-23: removed the entire dead ingress: block (namespace, gateway) from kubeflow-dashboard/values.yaml — not just the service_account key. Confirmed unused: zero .Values.ingress refs in any dashboard template (no VirtualService in this chart), and terraform helm_release kubeflow_dashboard doesn't override ingress.*. Dashboard is fronted by oauth2-proxy + a separate terraform kubernetes_ingress_v1, so the chart has no gateway binding to configure. Other charts' ingress blocks (pipelines, oauth2-proxy, istio-ingress) are actively consumed and untouched. helm template renders clean.
close_reason: DONE. Ingress-gateway SA reference is now standardized on the ingress.service_account value convention across all kubeflow charts that reference it. Part 1 (dead key): kubeflow-dashboard/values.yaml has zero ingress refs (dead block removed earlier). Part 2 (standardize): oauth2-proxy already used cluster.local/ns/{{.Values.ingress.namespace}}/sa/{{.Values.ingress.service_account}}; brought kubeflow-profiles/templates/ns-owner-access-waypoint.yaml (added later this session) into line — it had hardcoded sa/istio-ingressgateway. Added ingress.service_account: istio-ingressgateway to kubeflow-profiles/values.yaml and changed the policy to use {{ .Values.ingress.service_account }} (+ tidied the comment); bumped kubeflow-profiles 1.0.5->1.0.6. Render unchanged (still resolves to ns/ingress/sa/istio-ingressgateway). ml-pipeline-ui no longer references the gateway SA (platform-720 moved it to the oauth2-proxy SA). terraform doesn't override ingress.* for the profiles release, so the chart default applies; profiles release has no tf version pin (reads Chart.yaml). No hardcoded gateway-SA principals remain.

title: Verify Istio ambient vs kubeflow namespace istio-injection=enabled
description: istio/system installs the ambient profile (ztunnel/cni, PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT), but the kubeflow namespace is labeled istio-injection=enabled (sidecar). Confirm whether kubeflow should be ambient (remove the injection label / add to ambient mesh) or whether sidecar is intended for those workloads. Surfaced during README update; out of README scope.
notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-22: Istio is deliberately installed in AMBIENT mode (istio/system/terraform/main.tf: profile: ambient, PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT: true, istiod+cni+ztunnel, Istio 1.27.0). The ingress gateway is a sidecar Envoy (gateway chart in ns 'ingress', istio-injection=enabled) which is correct for ambient. BUG: workload namespaces are mislabeled for the dataplane — kubeflow/terraform/main.tf:9 sets kubeflow ns label 'istio-injection=enabled' (sidecar, no-op here) instead of 'istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient'; kubeflow-admin (profile-controller-generated) is likewise istio-injection=enabled and has NO dataplane-mode label. Result: workloads are only partially/inconsistently enrolled in ambient, so ztunnel L4 principal rules don't match peer identity (ml-pipeline-ui 403, platform-720) and L7 rules are silently dropped (ml-pipeline x-auth-request-email; profile ns-owner-access-istio). Gateway API CRDs are NOT installed -> waypoints currently impossible. Direction: commit to ambient (matches IaC). Migration: (1) install Gateway API CRDs; (2) relabel kubeflow + profile namespaces dataplane-mode=ambient, drop istio-injection; (3) restart workloads to enroll cleanly (fixes L4/principal); (4) add waypoints for L7-policy workloads (ml-pipeline, profile ns-owner-access-istio); (5) fix profile-controller gateway SA ref (platform-7d1: uses istio-system/sa/istio-ingressgateway-service-account, real is ingress/sa/istio-ingressgateway).
close_reason: Verified: cluster is deliberately Istio ambient (istio/system/terraform: profile ambient); kubeflow/kubeflow-admin were mislabeled istio-injection=enabled instead of dataplane-mode=ambient, causing the broken enrollment. PoC confirmed relabel+restart fixes it. Remediation tracked in platform-iyr (codify IaC) and platform-xd4 (waypoints/L7).

title: Rename root terragrunt.hcl to root.hcl (Terragrunt anti-pattern warning)
description: Terragrunt 0.86.2 warns that using terragrunt.hcl as the root config is an anti-pattern that will become an error in a future version. Rename root terragrunt.hcl -> root.hcl and update find_in_parent_folders()/include references across all units. Warning-only today; deferred from the 0.86.2 CLI flag migration (platform-1hv).

title: Harden mesh authz for out-of-mesh Kubeflow Pipelines callers (metadata/cache/minio)
description: metadata-grpc-service, service-cache-server, and minio-service AuthorizationPolicies effectively allow-all (- {}) because their callers have no Istio identity: metadata-envoy and Argo pipeline step pods run with sidecar.istio.io/inject=false, and cache-server's mutating webhook is invoked by kube-apiserver (outside the mesh). A principals allow-list would 403 them. Properly restricting requires an architectural change: inject sidecars into metadata-envoy + step pods and enable STRICT mTLS via PeerAuthentication, then scope each policy.
